Players have 20 minutes to play and restart the game as many times as they want. After 20 minutes pass, they can't restart anymore and have to play through completion or until they lose all their lives. Their score for the last run is then submitted to leaderboard.

Game Name: JoJo's Bizarre Adventure: All Star Battle R
Rules-Game Settings:
- Must be in the Discord.

- Timer: 99 seconds

- Rounds: 3

- Hazards: OFF

- Assists: ON

- Matches Format: Initially, all matches are Best of 3 (FT2). As we progress to Winners Finals, Losers Finals, and Grand Finals, the format shifts to Best of 5 (FT3).

- Stage Selection: We encourage Random Select for Stages; however, if both players mutually agree on a specific map, they are free to choose it.

- Reselection: Losers are allowed to switch Character, Assist, and Map; winners must stick with their choices.

- Connection: Wired ethernet only.

- "North America Only": Even though it says North America Only, other players that are not from North America can join and participate in the tournament. However, they are not allowed to host their computer on Parsec nor do Netplay; they can only join NA players hosting their computers or joining a Shadow.

- Game Ownership: It is recommended that you own JoJo's All-Star Battle R; however, all you really need is Parsec.

- Bans: Any glitches resulting in a softlock are forbidden. In the event of encountering such glitches, players must replay the match.
Info-Software Needed: Parsec, download from https://parsec.app/ and set up an account

Rules-Glitches:
Any bugs/glitches that happen will be handled with the same stock reset of the situation (Also goes for desyncs)

Rules-Game Settings:
3 stocks
8:00 minute timer
Aether Stages “OFF”
Turbo Mode “OFF”
Workshop "ON"
Rules-Notes:
- The opponent can ask for you to switch visual or sound cosmetics at any time and you must do it going into the next game. (If requested within the first 5 seconds of game startup, the game will just be restarted)

- Winner must alert loser of character-switching

- DSR, A player cannot return to any stage they have previously won on without the consent of their opponent 

-Although not required, we highly recommend connecting with an Ethernet cable. We'll try our best to be understanding with Wi-Fi, but players will be disqualified if their connection makes matches unplayable.

- Workshop Characters in the roster allowed ONLY

- Stages are chosen in game one using a 1-1-1-1 striking format (with the higher seed striking first), the winner of each game bans 3 stages, not counting those affected by DSR described in Section E.

Rules-Chara Change: Winner must keep character. Loser may switch.
Rules-Game Settings: - Pause: Hold
Rules-Notes:
Stage selection is random except by mutual agreement between players.

Loser of a match may either choose a stage or pick a different character for the next match. Winner must stay with the character they previously picked.

Draw Matches are not counted. In the event of a Draw, the Match must be replayed in the same conditions - same characters and same stage.

During Grand Finals, if the Player in the Loser's bracket wins, it sends the Player in Winners Bracket to Losers, triggering a "Reset". This Reset Match is considered a new Match for all intents and purposes, including character choice lock and stage randomization.

When beginning a set game 1 stage selection could be either random. Or if you and your opponent can agree on a game 1 stage selection you may go to that desired stage. if you and your opponent can not agree you go random

Winner keeps current character

Loser picks stage or character, not both; if loser picks character, stage returns to neutral

The loser of the prior game may switch stages (with the same character) or switch characters (with a random select stage )

Finals-Games per Set: 3/5 Games for Grand Finals, 3/5 Games for Winners Finals, 3/5 Games for Losers Finals
Rules-Chara Change: Winner must keep character. Loser may switch.
Rules-Glitches:
This is a rare but known bug which can occur with toss-ups on certain characters, most notably when Steve Miller is trying to catch the disc. These points are honored in tournament as the bug is very hard to reproduce and there is no easy way to discount these points without completely disrupting the match.

In the event that it happens, restart the match and try to replicate the score before the glitch. If this cannot be done, contact the TO/Bracket Runners.
Rules-Game Settings:
- First to 2 Wins,  TOP 4 FIRST TO 3. 
- First stage is BEACH. Loser counterpicks stage. 
- No Characters are banned. 
- Standard Settings apply (Set Count = 2, Winning Point  = 12, Timer = 99)
-  In case of Draw both players enter the same stage with same characters and first score wins the game (aka Extended Sudden Death)
- DSR is in play: You may not select a stage you have previously won on in the same set. 
- PLEASE ENSURE YOU ARE USING A WIRED CONNECTION. Wi-Fi connections are not acceptable. 
- All Matches must provide either a fightcade replay or a video replay in extreme cases. If no replay can be provided, Staff reserves the right to choose a player to advance.
- In the event that a match cannot be played or a lobby cannot be formed between players, inform the TO.
